question_text: Which of the following statements best explains why marathon runners need to consume more calories than people doing light activities?

options.label: A
options.text: Marathon running requires more glucose to be broken down in aerobic respiration, releasing more energy for muscle contraction over extended periods

options.label: B
options.text: Marathon runners' bodies are larger, so they need more food regardless of activity

options.label: C
options.text: Food consumed by runners converts to energy more efficiently than in other people

options.label: D
options.text: Marathon running causes food to be stored as energy instead of used immediately

explanation: During marathon running, cells respire more glucose aerobically (C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + energy) to meet the high energy demands of sustained muscle activity, requiring more food intake to replenish glucose stores.
